---
title: "Lab Component Snippets"
description: "Copy-ready fenced-div patterns for BER 640 lab rewrites."
---

This file is a source reference for authors. It is not listed in `_quarto.yml`, so it is not part of the student-facing book navigation.

## Objectives

Use once near the top of every lab.

````markdown
::: {.objectives}
#### What you'll be able to do

- Fit the model used in this lab.
- Inspect coefficients with `model_params()`.
- Evaluate fit or assumptions with `model_fit()` and `check_model()`.
- Translate the model into a prediction, contrast, slope, or diagnostic decision.

::: {.meta}
Lecture notes: Chapter X. Dataset: `dataset_name`. Estimated time: 60-90 minutes.
:::
:::
````

## Worked Example

Use for the main model output or a major post-estimation output. Keep the tabs short.

````markdown
::: {.worked-example}
::: {.wex-header}
[Worked example]{.wex-tag} - Inspect the model
:::

::: {.panel-tabset}
### Output

```{r}
#| label: labxx-model-parameters
model_params(model1)
```

### Output Interpretation

Write one or two sentences that interpret the main coefficient in substantive units.

### Code Explanation

- `lm()` or `glm()` fits the model.
- `model_params()` prints estimates, confidence intervals, test statistics, and p-values.
:::
:::
````

## Interpretation

Use immediately after an output that students need to translate.

````markdown
::: {.interpretation}
For students with the reference value of `group`, the predicted outcome is about X units.
The focal group differs by about Y units, holding the covariates in the model constant.
:::
````

## Your Turn

Use after a worked example. Keep it small enough to complete in a few minutes.

````markdown
::: {.your-turn}
[Your turn]{.yt-title} [R]{.badge-r}

Change one predictor value, refit one model, or compute one additional prediction.
Write one sentence describing what changed.

<details><summary>Hint</summary>
Start by copying the previous code chunk and changing one formula term or one value.
</details>
:::
````

## Assumption Check

Use in diagnostics sections. Require a decision, not just a plot.

````markdown
::: {.assumption-check}
State whether the diagnostic pattern is acceptable, questionable, or a serious concern.
Name the one plot or statistic that supports your decision, and say what you would do next.
:::
````

## Pitfall

Use for one concrete mistake and one corrected habit.

````markdown
::: {.pitfall}
Do not interpret a logistic-regression coefficient as an odds ratio unless it has been exponentiated.
Use `model_params(model, exponentiate = TRUE)` when you want odds ratios.
:::
````

## What To Submit

Use once near the end of every lab.

````markdown
::: {.exercises}
### What to submit

1. A coefficient table for the main model. [core]{.chip .med}
2. One prediction, contrast, slope, or diagnostic table/figure. [core]{.chip .med}
3. A 3-5 sentence interpretation that answers the research question. [core]{.chip .med}
4. One diagnostic note that names the main assumption or fit concern. [warm-up]{.chip .easy}
:::
````

## Summary

Use at the end of every lab. Do not introduce new content here.

````markdown
::: {.summary}
### Lab summary

::: {.summary-grid}
::: {}
##### Key takeaways
- Fit:
- Inspect:
- Predict or compare:
- Diagnose:
:::
::: {}
##### Functions used
- `lm()` or `glm()`
- `model_params()`
- `model_fit()`
- `check_model()`
:::
:::
:::
````
